title: Iran War Live Updates: Israel Appears to Pull Back From Threat to Strike Beirut
summary: President Trump is pressing Israel not to attack the Lebanese capital. An escalation in Israel’s campaign against Hezbollah could threaten peace talks with Iran.

title: Trump Backs Off Plan for $1.8 Billion Fund That Drew Political Backlash
summary: The fund has drawn backlash from critics who said it was a scheme to reward President Trump’s political allies with public benefits.

title: Losing Trust in Justice Dept., Judges Call Out Its Lawyers’ Behavior
summary: The federal courts have long assumed that the government’s lawyers are trustworthy. Now judges across the country are criticizing their lack of candor.

title: Trump Targets Brazil With 25% Tariff, Citing Unfair Trade Practices
summary: The proposal marks the latest effort by the administration to rebuild its tariff agenda through Section 301 investigations.

title: Trump Finds High-Wire Iran Negotiations ‘Very Boring’
summary: President Trump told CNBC that he “couldn’t care less” if the negotiations with Iran break down.

title: Security Council Members Call for Israel to Withdraw From Southern Lebanon
summary: The U.N. panel met after Israel had threatened to attack southern Beirut. President Trump said later that Israel and Hezbollah had agreed not to attack each other.

title: U.S. Says More People May Be Charged in Terror Case Linked to Iran
summary: A militia leader pleaded not guilty on Monday to what prosecutors say is his role in attacks that are part of Iran’s response to the war with Israel and the United States.

title: After an Agonizing Week of Threats, Kyiv Is Finally Bombarded by Russia
summary: Moscow’s repeated warnings of a major strike, combined with the delay before it happened, seemed intended to inflict a psychological toll on the Ukrainian capital.

title: Anthropic Files to Go Public, Setting Stage for Huge I.P.O.
summary: The artificial intelligence company, which is racing OpenAI to the stock market, has seen explosive growth over the last year thanks largely to technology that can automatically write computer code.

title: California’s Public Universities Went All in on A.I. Now They’re Tearing Themselves Apart.
summary: California’s public universities spent $16.9 million on A.I. during a financial crisis, and the result has been chaos.

title: Scott Pelley Accuses CBS News Boss of ‘Murdering’ ‘60 Minutes’
summary: In an explosive staff meeting, Mr. Pelley, a correspondent for the long-running Sunday news show, blasted Bari Weiss, the CBS editor in chief, and Nick Bilton, the show’s new executive producer.

title: The Return of Blaming and Shaming in Public Health
summary: For years, medicine has tried to eliminate stigma. Now, Robert F. Kennedy Jr. is bringing back the language of personal responsibility.

title: Minnesota Republicans Hold Moment of Silence for Derek Chauvin
summary: Delegates to the Minnesota Republican Party’s convention voted to hold a moment of silence for Derek Chauvin, who was convicted of murdering George Floyd.

title: Obesity May Be More Prevalent Than Previously Thought
summary: A new study suggests using body mass index alone may miss many people with the disease, but the findings come with caveats.

title: Mills Reminds Maine Voters She’s ‘Still on the Ballot’ for Senate Amid Platner Controversy
summary: A month after withdrawing from the state’s Senate race, Gov. Janet Mills suggested she remained an option after the likely Democratic nominee, Graham Platner, faced a new scandal.

title: Previewing California’s Primary Elections and High-Stakes Governor’s Race
summary: The deep-blue state’s primary elections will show how angry voters are at the status quo.

title: As Ebola Spreads, Scientists Race to Find Vaccines and Treatments
summary: The Bundibugyo virus, a little known type, previously had caused just two small outbreaks. Now it’s at the center of a rapidly widening epidemic in Africa.

title: What to Know About the Ebola Outbreak
summary: Aid agencies are racing to help health workers in the Democratic Republic of Congo. The virus is known to have killed at least 43, but the true toll may be far higher.

title: The New Zealand Parakeet Pair That Are Saving Their Species
summary: There are about 450 orange-fronted parakeets left in the world. Super breeders Nacho and Trixie are doing their best to bolster the population.

title: Marilyn Monroe Fans Descended on Palm Springs For Her 100th Birthday
summary: To honor her centennial, over 1,000 Marilyn Monroe fans came together to dress as the screen legend in Palm Springs, where her Hollywood career began.

title: Why America Is Its Own Biggest Geopolitical Risk
summary: The political risk analyst Ian Bremmer argues that the greatest driver of risk in the world right now is President Trump.

title: Late Night Updates America on the Lineup for Its 250th Birthday Bash
summary: “Instead of music, the entertainment will be an 80-year-old man yelling about windmills,” Jimmy Kimmel said after President Trump said he would headline a summer concert series.

title: Rick Adelman, Hall of Fame N.B.A. Coach, Dies at 79
summary: His teams made the playoffs 16 times in 23 seasons, and he twice steered the Portland Trail Blazers to the league finals.
